name: Padmapada
dates_notes: Direct disciple of Еҡaб№…kara per tradition; only the first few pДҒdas of his PaГұcapДҒdikДҒ survive.
core_thesis: PadmapДҒda, Еҡaб№…kara's direct disciple, is the founding figure of what becomes the *Vivaraб№Үa* sub-school of Advaita. He takes the *adhyДҒsa-bhДҒб№Јya* and the opening *catuбёҘsЕ«trД«* of Еҡaб№…kara's *Brahma-SЕ«tra-BhДҒб№Јya* (BSB 1.1.1вҖ“4) and develops them into a positive ontology of *avidyДҒ*. The decisive move is at *PaГұcapДҒdikДҒ* VвҖ“VI, where he glosses the compound *mithyДҒjГұДҒna* as *mithyДҒ ca tad ajГұДҒnaб№ғ ca* and identifies the *ajГұДҒna*-component with a *jaбёҚДҒtmikДҒ avidyДҒЕӣakti* вҖ” an ignorance-power whose nature is insentient вҖ” that must be admitted in connection with all internal and external things, otherwise the appearance of false objects would be impossible. *AvidyДҒ* (also called *mДҒyДҒ* and *mЕ«la-prakб№ӣti*) is therefore a positive, beginningless (*anДҒdi*) *bhДҒva-rЕ«pa* entity, not mere absence of knowledge (*PaГұcapДҒdikДҒ* VвҖ“VI). Its locus (*ДҒЕӣraya*) and object (*viб№Јaya*) are both *brahman* (*PaГұcapДҒdikДҒ* VIIвҖ“VIII). It possesses two *Еӣaktis*: *ДҒvaraб№Үa* (concealment of *brahman*'s *svarЕ«pa*) and *vikб№Јepa* (projection of the manifest world) (*PaГұcapДҒdikДҒ* IXвҖ“X). The *jД«va* is a *pratibimba* (reflection) of *brahman* in the mirror of *avidyДҒ* (*PaГұcapДҒdikДҒ* XXIX.108, on *tat tvam asi*) вҖ” the structural alternative to the *avaccheda-vДҒda* (limitation account) that the *BhДҒmatД«* line inherits from Maб№ҮбёҚana (*Brahma-Siddhi* 1.1, opening prose). PadmapДҒda's positions on *avidyДҒ*'s positivity, on *brahman* as its substrate, and on *pratibimba* are foundational for PrakДҒЕӣДҒtman's *Vivaraб№Үa* and the entire later *Vivaraб№Үa* tradition.

engaged_works.title: Pancapadika
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored for the surviving portion (first five pДҒdas of Еҡaб№…kara's Brahma-SЕ«tra-BhДҒб№Јya).

ascription_evidence.description: Modern academic consensus on PadmapДҒda's authorship of the surviving portion.

engaged_works.summary: *б№¬Д«kДҒ* on Еҡaб№…kara's *Brahma-SЕ«tra-BhДҒб№Јya*. The text-historical situation is unusual and critical. Only a portion survives: the commentary on the first five *pДҒdas* of the BSB вҖ” that is, the *adhyДҒsa-bhДҒб№Јya* and the *catuбёҘsЕ«trД«* (BSB 1.1.1вҖ“4), plus a small additional stretch. The work's title literally means "commentary on five *pДҒdas*." The traditional account holds that PadmapДҒda completed a *б№ӯД«kДҒ* on the entire BSB but the manuscript was destroyed, and that Еҡaб№…kara reconstructed what could be reconstructed from oral memory. Whatever the historical truth of that narrative, the surviving portion is doctrinally weight-bearing precisely because it covers the BSB's most metaphysically dense passages вҖ” the *adhyДҒsa-bhДҒб№Јya* and the analysis of *tat tu samanvayДҒt*. Three positions developed in this stretch are taken up wholesale by PrakДҒЕӣДҒtman in the *PaГұcapДҒdikДҒ-Vivaraб№Үa*: *avidyДҒ* as *bhДҒva-rЕ«pa* (PaГұcapДҒdikДҒ VвҖ“VI), the refined *adhyДҒsa* definition as *paratra pЕ«rvadб№ӣб№Јб№ӯДҒvabhДҒsaбёҘ smб№ӣtirЕ«paбёҘ* вҖ” "the appearance elsewhere, in the form of memory, of what was previously seen" (XIX.62вҖ“63) вҖ” and the *bimba-pratibimba* reading of *tat tvam asi* (XXIX.108). The *PaГұcapДҒdikДҒ-Vivaraб№Үa* becomes the founding text of the sub-school. Securely authored for the surviving portion.

key_passages.text_critical_note: The dispatched local corpus is an English translation; this Sanskrit sentence is transcribed from a secondary scholarly citation of the PaГұcapДҒdikДҒ locus and normalized only for obvious sandhi spacing.

samasa_vigrahas.resolution: mithyДҒ ca tad ajГұДҒnaб№ғ ca iti mithyДҒjГұДҒnam
samasa_vigrahas.note: PadmapДҒda himself explicitly decomposes the expression this way.
The internal qualifier jaбёҚДҒtmikДҒ functions karmadhДҒraya-like within the larger tatpuruб№Јa.
Exclusion or negation with respect to knowledge.
BahuvrД«hi-like semantics are compressed into a determinative expression.
Again, the sense is 'having that as material cause'.
A practice or transaction dependent on an occasioning cause.
The power adheres only to sheer existence, not to a fully articulated real essence.

karaka_structure.note: Nominative subject of the passive ucyate in the gloss on mithyДҒ.
Nominative subject of the passive ucyate in the gloss on ajГұДҒna.
Entity to be admitted in abhyupagantavyДҒ.
Domain in which the avidyДҒ-Еӣakti must be admitted.

key_passages.english_close: "MithyДҒjГұДҒna" is that which is both mithyДҒ and ajГұДҒna. By the term "mithyДҒ", indefinability is stated. And by the term "ajГұДҒna", the power of avidyДҒ вҖ” whose own nature is insentient. It is stated, by way of the exclusion of knowledge. "Having that as its cause" means: having that as its material cause. How then can a transactional practice that has an occasioning cause be beginningless by nature? Here it is said as follows. This very power of avidyДҒ must necessarily be admitted. With respect to external and internal things, it must be admitted as connected only with the mere existence that is the own-nature of those things. Otherwise, the manifestation of false objects would be impossible.

preserved_terms.first_use_gloss: indefinable ignorance; not mere false judgment but ignorance construed as the stuff of superimposition
the power of ignorance, treated here as a positive explanatory factor

key_passages.why_this_passage: This is not a famous isolated slogan; it is the place where PadmapДҒda explicitly glosses mithyДҒjГұДҒna as an avidyДҒ-Еӣakti and immediately argues that empirical superimposition is therefore beginningless yet causally grounded. That is the decisive doctrinal move from Еҡaб№…kara's adhyДҒsa-language toward the later Vivaraб№Үa-style positive ontology of avidyДҒ, and the choice is constrained by the dispatched local corpus being an English translation only.
key_passages.source_edition: PadmapДҒda, PaГұcapДҒdikДҒ V.15вҖ“VI.16; locus selected against D. Venkataramiah, trans., The Pancapadika of Padmapada (Baroda: Oriental Institute, 1948), pp. 11вҖ“12; Sanskrit wording recovered from a scholarly citation of the PaГұcapДҒdikДҒ because the dispatched local corpus is an English translation.

pada_analysis.gloss: the appearing of what was seen before
appearance having the form of memory
the appearing of what was seen before

samasa_vigrahas.note: The appearance is memory-like, not straightforward recollection.

key_passages.english_close: The appearance elsewhere of what was seen before; the appearance elsewhere of what has the form of memory; [thus, adhyДҒsa is] the appearance elsewhere, in the form of memory, of what was previously seen.

preserved_terms.first_use_gloss: superimposition; the erroneous placing of one thing's content in another
memory-formed; presenting itself in the mode of recollection without being literal recollection

key_passages.why_this_passage: This definition is the load-bearing epistemic core of PadmapДҒda's long adhyДҒsa analysis. It fairly represents his position because the surrounding discussion tests and refines each limb of the formula rather than merely repeating Еҡaб№…kara's opening phrase, and the local translation volume preserves the Sanskrit definition itself in transliteration.
key_passages.source_edition: PadmapДҒda, PaГұcapДҒdikДҒ XIX.62вҖ“63, definition as transliterated in D. Venkataramiah, trans., The Pancapadika of Padmapada (Baroda: Oriental Institute, 1948), pp. 43вҖ“44.

key_passages.locus_long: PaГұcapДҒdikДҒ, XXIX.108, reading tat tvam asi through the bimba-pratibimba relation

karaka_structure.note: Subject of asi in the mahДҒvДҒkya.
Subject in the counterfactual negated reading na tvam asi.

key_passages.english_close: [The mahДҒvДҒkya is] "tat tvam asi". It would not [merely amount to] "na tat tvam asi" вҖ” "that you are not". Rather, it would amount to "na tvam asi" вҖ” "you yourself are not". [And that absurdity would be] just like "there is no silver".

preserved_terms.first_use_gloss: reflection; the image-side of the jД«va in PadmapДҒda's model
prototype; the original of which the jД«va is the reflection

key_passages.why_this_passage: This is the point where the reflection model is made to do semantic work on the mahДҒvДҒkya itself, not merely illustrative work. It therefore fairly represents PadmapДҒda's account of the jД«va-Brahman relation and of liberating recognition through tat tvam asi; the choice is again constrained by the dispatched local corpus being an English translation.
key_passages.source_edition: PadmapДҒda, PaГұcapДҒdikДҒ XXIX.108; locus selected against D. Venkataramiah, trans., The Pancapadika of Padmapada (Baroda: Oriental Institute, 1948), p. 74; Sanskrit clause verified from a secondary citation of PP 22.21вҖ“23 because the dispatched local corpus is an English translation.
